items.title: How startup Propy is deploying $100 million to put real estate deals on the blockchain - CNBC
items.summary: # How startup Propy is deploying $100 million to put real estate deals on the blockchain. * Propy, a Miami-based blockchain technology, has been trying to modernize the closing process for residential homes. * Propy is also using its new funding to develop an AI agent to help facilitate deals. Propy, a Miami-based blockchain technology company that launched in 2017, has been trying to modernize the closing process and recently got a big financial boost to do it. "We have this conviction that blockchain is the next phenomenon after the internet," said Natalia Karayaneva, founder and CEO of Propy. Meet Avery Propy is also using its new funding to develop an AI agent to help facilitate deals. "Real estate developers and REITs started to reach out to Propy, because we've established this brand for so many years with our strong conviction that there was a way to legally accept cryptocurrencies in real estate, to legally record these on blockchain and in the county, of course," said Karayaneva.

items.title: KBank partners Ant International to build blockchain USD rail in Thailand - FinTech Futures
items.summary: # KBank partners Ant International to build blockchain USD rail in Thailand. The project will use JP Morgan’s Kinexys blockchain architecture to process 24/7 cross-border USD transactions and manage regional liquidity. Kasikornbank (KBank) has inked a new memorandum of understanding to develop cross-border payment and liquidity infrastructure for. A joint statement says the project will "combine KBank’s regulated financial capabilities with Ant International’s financial AI solutions" to offer cross-border USD transactions with real-time settlement. A third partner, JP Morgan Payments, is providing the project with blockchain deposit accounts through its blockchain business. This latest blockchain settlement model outlined in their most recent MoU builds directly upon the Project Carina pilot from 2024, when KBank and Ant International used the same JP Morgan architecture to test internal cross-border remittance mechanisms. Tyler Pathe is a financial reporter, moderator and investigator with a specific interest in financial technology, banking and the financial industry's latest innovations.

items.title: Blockchain Reputation Is Evolving Beyond Transactions, Trust Now Follows Activity Patterns - Qoo Media
items.summary: # Blockchain Reputation Is Evolving Beyond Transactions, Trust Now Follows Activity Patterns. blockchain digital reputation trust network / Img: nature.com. In Web3, credibility is no longer judged only by what an account claims to be. It is increasingly shaped by what can be observed on-chain, where public activity records give communities a clearer way to read trust and behavior. That shift is changing how digital reputation works across blockchain ecosystems. A profile name or digital identity still matters, but consistent activity, verifiable participation, and interaction patterns are now becoming equally important signals. In this environment, certain activities can be seen and verified publicly, which gives digital identity a more legible character for the community. A user’s consistency, the way they engage in a network, and the history of their interactions now help shape how credible that account appears to others. This is part of a broader change in how blockchain data is understood.

items.title: [2301.00001] NFTrig
items.summary:
# Computer Science > Human-Computer Interaction **arXiv:2301.00001** (cs) \[Submitted on 21 Dec 2022\] # Title:NFTrig Authors: [Jordan Thompson](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Thompson,+J), [Ryan Benac](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Benac,+R), [Kidus Olana](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Olana,+K), [Talha Hassan](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Hassan,+T), [Andrew Sward](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Sward,+A), [Tauheed Khan Mohd](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Mohd,+T+K) View a PDF of the paper titled NFTrig, by Jordan Thompson and 5 other authors [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2301.00001) > Abstract:NFTrig is a web-based application created for use as an educational tool to teach trigonometry and block chain technology. Creation of the application includes front and back end development as well as integration with other outside sources including MetaMask and OpenSea. The primary development languages include HTML, CSS (Bootstrap 5), and JavaScript as well as Solidity for smart contract creation. The application itself is hosted on Moralis utilizing their Web3 API. This technical report describes how the application was created, what the application requires, and smart contract design with security considerations in mind. The NFTrig application has underwent significant testing and validation prior to and after deployment. Future suggestions and recommendations for further development, maintenance, and use in other fields for education are also described. | | | | --- | --- | | Subjects: | Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC) | | Cite as: | [arXiv:2301.00001](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.00001) \[cs.HC\] | | | (or [arXiv:2301.00001v1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.00001v1) \[cs.HC\] for this version) | | | [https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2301.00001](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2301.00001)
Focus to learn more

items.title: ChatGPT—The Fastest-Growing App Ever—Is Losing Downloads As Claude Surges - Forbes
items.summary: OpenAI’s ChatGPT, still the clear leader among AI chatbots, has lost a chunk of its market share in terms of global app downloads over the past year as fast-growing competitor Claude has surged, according to data from market intelligence firm Sensor Tower shared with Forbes. Sensor Tower, which measured downloads of six leading AI apps, found ChatGPT accounted for 47% of downloads of all these apps globally in the second quarter of 2026 so far, below its prior level of dominance—in the second quarter of 2025, ChatGPT accounted for 67% of all AI app downloads, according to insights analyst Kara Lee. In its infancy, ChatGPT quickly became the fastest-growing consumer app within two months of launch, garnering 100 million users by January 2023. ChatGPT’s monthly active users are up 67% year-over-year as of the second quarter of 2026 so far, Sensor Tower found, but its growth is much lower than Claude, which has surged 627% year-over-year in terms of monthly active users, according to Sensor Tower.

items.title: OpenAI is building a new kind of ad machine. Early data suggests Google should worry. - Business Insider
items.summary: # OpenAI is building a new kind of ad machine. OpenAI's move into advertising is starting to look like the foundation of a massive new revenue engine, one that could challenge Google's search empire in significant ways. That's according to new data from Similarweb executive Heral Amir, who's been studying early ChatGPT ads bought by hundreds of companies, including HubSpot, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nordstrom, Cursor, and Indeed. Traditional Google Search advertising depends on users typing commercially valuable phrases like "best running shoes" or "cheap flights to Miami." ChatGPT, by contrast, can infer intent from a sprawling back-and-forth exchange, even when the user never explicitly searches for a product. The context of a rich conversation creates an ad opportunity, not a Search query with specific keywords, according to Amir's analysis. That involved 10 turns of the conversation with ChatGPT, before an ad from Dick's Sporting Goods appeared. According to Similarweb's data, 46% of ChatGPT users who eventually saw an ad started with no commercial intent.

items.title: Global AI boom comes with a power bill: Inside ChatGPT’s explosive growth - Digital Journal
items.summary: # Global AI boom comes with a power bill: Inside ChatGPT’s explosive growth. As AI tools are constantly improving, appealing to an increasing number of users, ChatGPT remains the most popular chatbot – but just how popular and how much energy does it consume? With an estimated 900 million weekly active users and roughly 1.17 trillion prompts processed annually, OpenAI’s flagship chatbot now operates at a scale comparable to the largest digital platforms. A new analysis from the firm BestBrokers, reviewed by *Digital Journal,* offers a striking snapshot of how global demand for AI is distributed—and what it costs in computational terms. The findings highlight two key trends shaping the AI era: the rapid rise of emerging markets and the growing energy footprint of large-scale AI systems. While the United States remains a dominant force in the tech ecosystem, it is no longer the largest user of ChatGPT.

items.title: 81% of ChatGPT-Cited Brands Don't Rank on Google: New Study Released - markets.businessinsider.com
items.summary: # 81% of ChatGPT-Cited Brands Don't Rank on Google: New Study Released. *EMGI study reveals 81% of ChatGPT-cited brands don't rank in Google's top 10, highlighting decoupling between AI citation and traditional search ranking. Marketing agencies invest in proprietary AI content infrastructure to produce consensus signal across multiple sources.*. Dublin, Ireland, May 30, 2026 -- A 2026 analysis of 150 SaaS companies has revealed that 81% of brands recommended by ChatGPT do not rank in Google's top 10 for the same queries, signaling a substantive decoupling between AI citation and traditional search engine ranking. Marketing agencies responding to this shift are investing in what industry analysts describe as proprietary AI content infrastructure: a three-layer system designed to produce consensus signal at scale. As AI engines increasingly determine which brands achieve visibility, the ability to engineer consensus signal across multiple sources has become the competitive moat that separates agencies capable of delivering measurable AI citation performance from those still optimizing for a single-channel ranking model.

items.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman over ChatGPT - Axios
items.summary: # Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman over ChatGPT. Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Monday sued OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman, alleging that the company marketed ChatGPT to the public without sufficiently warning of its dangers. **Why it matters**:Florida is the first state to sue OpenAI, part of a growing string of lawsuits the state has filed against Big Tech companies, including Meta and Snapchat. **Driving the news**: The complaint argues that OpenAI ignored warnings from experts about ChatGPT, which the state says has "aided and abetted" mass shooters and encouraged vulnerable individuals toward suicide. * Among the examples of ChatGPT's harms cited in the complaint is the murder of two University of South Florida graduate students, in which authorities say the suspect sought the tool's advice on disposing of bodies. * In April, Uthmeier opened a criminal investigation into OpenAI after it was revealed that the gunman who opened fire at Florida State University had consulted ChatGPT before the shooting.

items.title: A random 14-year-old American girl bought a house in cash at 16 while her classmates were taking exams.
items.summary:
A random 14-year-old American girl bought a house in cash at 16 while her classmates were taking exams.

It started with one Google Maps search - she found an HVAC company with 4.8 stars, 280 reviews and a website too embarrassing to show clients.

Copied their data into ChatGPT 5.5 -> 2 minutes later a complete brief, an hour later a live site with booking and a reviews section.

Called the owner and showed him the preview. He signed the $900 invoice the same day.

Then she stopped looking for clients by hand. 

AI pulls 200 businesses from Google Maps in 10 minutes - name, email, rating, review count.

ChatGPT 5.5 writes a personalized email for each one with their real business data and emails them automatically. 

500 emails a day, 3% respond, 10% of those become clients.

Month one - $3,800. 
Month six - $22,000. 
Two years - $380,000 and a house.

Five million businesses on Google Maps are still waiting for that call.

Start your software factory now.

they transfer their memory, open a new chat, ask their first question
and think they're set

but memory import is just 1 of 10 features ChatGPT doesn't have

full breakdown in the article below

items.title: > the pipeline behind "AI dancing girl" accounts:
items.summary:
> the pipeline behind "AI dancing girl" accounts:

1. find a viral tiktok dance, download it
2. screenshot frame 1 → chatgpt writes the prompt
3. generate your model from it (freepik)
4. wavespeed → kling 2.6 motion control
5. add image + dance video → run
6. your model does the viral dance
7. post 2x/day → traffic → fanvue

> the tech is real
> the "$7,500 a day" is bait for the paid group
> they're not selling the model. they're selling the funnel
